source_name: Code4rena Ethena Labs Findings and Analysis Report (2024-11) - Low Risk and Non-Critical Issues
issue_claim: The _beforeTokenTransfer() function does not verify whether addresses are whitelisted when WHITELIST_ENABLED is set.
why_it_looked_bad: As noted in line 191, the function only checks if the address to is not blacklisted. Consequently, unwhitelisted users can still receive UStb when WHITELIST_ENABLED is active. This issue appears in several locations.
why_not_valid: _beforeTokenTransfer whitelist check gap is related to role-state consistency, but the final report classified it as low absent a stronger value-loss path
downgrade_reason: _beforeTokenTransfer whitelist check gap is related to role-state consistency, but the final report classified it as low absent a stronger value-loss path
trust_assumption: Treat as a caution/false-positive reference unless the current target shows permissionless reachability, direct value loss, and concrete affected code path.
when_it_could_be_real: if combined with permissionless access or large-value state transitions
if the affected path becomes critical to mint/redeem/withdrawal solvency or availability
